rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF AVA LOUISE VASQUEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/5/23 for an appearance. Other issue(s) necessitating continuance and/or to be addressed at the hearing on 7/5/23: (1) The "courtesy copy" received by the examiner does not appear to be a true and correct copy; it is unsigned. There is also no Summary of Account attached to it. (2) Attorney fees previously ordered but not yet paid ARE a liability of the estate. The definition of a liability is something that is owed; the fact that the funds are in the estate account means the estate still owes those fees. (3) Guardian does not address when, "previously", the SF Fire Credit Union accounts "set up" and why those funds were not blocked. (4) Guardian has maintained funds in excess of the FDIC insured amount in one account. (5) Filing account statements showing the account balance as of the closing date of the court accounting is required by law, Prob. C. Sect. 2620(c)(2), not merely as a convenience to the court. (6) The attorney billed to travel to the Court on 10/5/21 to file bank records. (7) Attorney costs do not show WHAT was filed or sent via messenger on 6/8/21 and 7/7/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CONNIE M. MARATAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/7/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The pleading caption is missing the name of the conservatee. (2) The amended notice showed a hearing date of 6/7/23, not this hearing on 6/21/23. (3) Schedule C does not describe the $6,513.93 "withdrawal" made on 4/2/19. (4) Schedule C also does not state the date of any order approving "caretaker expense" to the conservator and to Ted Maratas. (5) No fee declaration by the former attorney, Jennifer Marlowe, appears to have been filed contemporaneously with this petition (on 3/7/23) as alleged in paragraph 25; it was not attached to this petition and no copy of a separately filed fee declaration has been received by the assigned examiner. (6) There is also no separate fee declaration for the current attorney's fees mentioned in paragraphs 27 and 29 of this petition. (7) There is no Exhibit B attached to this petition, as alleged in paragraph 32. The ending balances have not been verified yet, as numerous statements were filed, both ostensibly in original form (they are not yet in the Confidential file) and as copies, where only the ending balances of the accounting needed to be verified. Hopefully, the ending balance of assets in this accounting ending 7/17/19 will be the same as the beginning balance of assets on hand in the general conservatorâs amended first account. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/27/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN EUGENE CAMPBELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) Receipts for any preliminary distribution must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.c.] (3) The Fidelity and Morgan Stanley accounts were clearly not cash if they were liquidated. They must be appraised by the Probate Referee. [Probate Code Section 8902.] (4) What happened to the household furniture, furnishings, and personal [e]ffects appraised at $500? (5) The accounting by a personal representative must begin on the date of death. (6) Refunds for amounts paid out of the estate or receipts reclaimed during estate administration are not receipts includable in the fee base. (7) An accounting should have four columns showing the date of a transaction, the source or payee, the nature or purpose, and the amount. The accounting is rather vague. Any disbursements not fully described must be deducted from the administrator's compensation. (8) The gains and losses schedules do not show dates of the transactions. To whom were the vehicles sold? (9) Administrator has kept cash in excess of the FDIC insured amount in one (failed) bank. (Administrator alleges that she has the original statements. The attorney alleges the same.) (10) It appears that the filing fee for the petition for preliminary distribution was already paid out of the estate, so it should not be reimbursed now to the attorney. (11) Please file the seller's closing statement to verify the costs of sale. The account and petition must be AMENDED after the Probate Referee appraises the Fidelity and Morgan Stanley accounts. If they were truly just cash accounts, please file the statements immediately preceding the date of death evidencing that. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Letters of Administration, not Testamentary, were issued to the administrator. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SUEKO HONIG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar, whether the conservator should be suspended, and/or whether an order to show cause should be issued re monetary sanctions for the conservator's failure to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 02/23/2023): 1) The bond has not been increased. 2) No original statement of the Union Bank account #8309 verifying the 11/17/2020 balance is on file. 4) The total charges do not match the total credits. 5) Schedule A-1 shows distribution of $50,612.67 received from the deceased spouse's conservator, but Schedule C-1 to the deceased spouse's conservator's supplemental accounting shows distribution of $50,689.71 to this conservatorship. 6) Please state the purpose of the $90 payment to Kie Taylor on 01/06/2021 reported on Schedule C. 7) Schedule C subtotals do not match the total amount of disbursements. 8) The petitioner's services between 09/04/2020 and 11/16/2020 described on Exhibit B appear to be related to the conservatorship of the person, rather than the conservatorship of the estate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARVIN HONIG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar and/or whether an order to show cause should be issued re monetary sanctions for the conservator's failure to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 02/23/2023): 1) No original statement of the Union Bank account #8309 verifying the 11/17/2020 balance is on file. 3) No original statement of the Union Bank account #8309 verifying the 01/31/2022 balance is on file. The statement filed in the spouse's conservatorship case shows a difference balance. 4) Schedule C-1 does not show SSA/VA/pension payments reported to have been distributed to the spouse's conservator on 12/03/2020, 12/31/2020, 02/01/2021, 02/03/2021, 03/01/2021 & 03/03/2021. See Schedule A & Schedule A-1 to the spouse's conservator's accounting. 5) The petitioner's services between 09/04/2020 and 11/16/2020 described on Exhibit B appear to be related to the conservatorship of the person, rather than the conservatorship of the estate. 6) No spousal property order appears to have been issued for the Union Bank account #8216, Bank of America account, US Bank account, and East West Bank account.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE MARGARET TILLERY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/24/2023 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 05/16/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) The petition must be amended and renoticed to request exoneration of the petitioners' bond as additional relief sought (unless the petitioners intend to apply ex parte for final discharge after this petition is adjudicated). A receipt signed by the successor administrator must be filed before the petitioners' bond can be exonerated on this petition. 2) Schedule C shows payments to Lucinda Lee in the total sum of $48,109.68, but Schedule A only shows reimbursement of $46,436.38. 3) Despite the allegation that the estate cash has been kept in interest-bearing accounts, Schedule A does not show any interest payment. 4) The ending cash balance of the E*Trade account on Schedule E does not match the balance that can readily be ascertained from the account statement. No interest and dividend paid between the date of death and 10/31/22 were reported on Schedule A. 5) The E*Trade statement shows 25 shares of Warner Bros Discovery, but those shares are not listed on Schedule E, nor is there an additional schedule reporting the purchase(s). 6) The ending cash balance of the Chase account exceeded the FDIC limit. 7) The attorney's "declaration" filed on 5/15/23 must include language required by CCP section 2015.5.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KALVIN M. MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although there were still numerous issues with the "Second Amended First and Final Distribution" petition filed on 5/17/23: (1) Letters were issued on 6/21/22, not 6/20/22; (2) the second sentence of paragraph 10 makes no sense; (3) there are references in the petition (e.g., paragraph 14) and in the disclaimers to "the governing instrument" but there is no governing instrument, as the decedent died intestate; (4) paragraph 16 refers to an account, but accounting was waived; (5) paragraph 16 also mentions that the estate account was just opened as of May 11, 2023, when the estate has been open since 1/20/22; (6) paragraph 14 is erroneous if all cash - and the estate reportedly consisted of only cash - was invested in interest bearing accounts, but the cash on hand is still $213,713.06, the same as on the date of death; (7) both the prayer and verification of this second amended petition refer to the wrong pleading; and (8) the prayer is missing the attorney fee. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] No conformed copy of the order will be returned as no SASE was provided.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN K. NAKAMOTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 11/6/23. A petition for final distribution or a second report of status must be filed by 10/26/23. Appearance will be required on 11/6/23 if no petition is timely filed. (When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.] If a hearing is continued or postponed, no further notice of the continued or postponed hearing is required unless ordered by the court. [Probate Code Section 1205.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HITOSHI OHE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Although file stamped on 6/6/23, the amended petition was not e-filed until 6/8/23, when it was first available to be viewed on the Court's website. Notice of the 8/2/23 hearing must be given at least 15 days before 8/2/23. (2) Furthermore, the Corrected I&A was not filed until 6/9/23, not 6/6/23, as alleged in paragraph 9. (The replacement check was not actually a date of death value of the John Hancock Fujitsu 401(k) plan, but this will be disregarded in this instance.) (3) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (4) Paragraph 20 states the incorrect amount of statutory compensation to be paid to the administrator (otherwise correctly shown by the numerals in paragraphs 19 and 20), and the amount shown in the proposed distribution (Attachment 6) is also incorrect. (5) The net amount to be distributed, as shown on Attachment 6, is incorrect. (6) Attorney costs reflect e-filing costs for proposed orders and the DE-121 for the continued hearing date. Proposed orders should NOT be e-filed; they are required by this Court to be provided in hard copy form. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] There is no statutory requirement to provide notice of a continued hearing. [Probate Code Section 1205.] Attorney costs should be reduced by $20.00. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/21/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERT A. PATERSON, JR. AND CHRISTINE E. PATERSON TRUST DATED AUGUST 24, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Determining Validity Of Trust Amendment
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HERCULES MOSS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Status Report And Petition For Termination Of Trust And Discharge Of Bond
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/02/2023, as the following issues (see tentative ruling for 05/10/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) Re the DE-120 form filed on 05/24/2023: i) The CourtCall information listed on the form is incorrect. ii) Item 1 identified the guardian ad litem as the petitioner, but this petition was filed by the trustee. iii) Notice must be given to the Departments of Developmental Services and State Hospitals at their current addresses (see DE-120 form filed on 03/24/2023). New notice must be given. iv) Notice must be given to the guardian ad litem who is not joining in this petition. 2) Did the guardian ad litem and petitioner execute the joinder agreement prior to the beneficiary's death? If not, has the beneficiary's sub-account with the master pooled trust ever been established so as to require termination thereof? 3) Has the $103,000 bond ever been issued to the petitioner so as to require exoneration thereof? 4) It is unclear whether the petition intended to request termination of the master pooled trust in its entirety or the deceased beneficiary's sub-acccount without affecting the master pooled trust. If the master pooled trust is sought to be terminated, all other beneficiaries whose sub-accounts have been established must be identified and receive notice.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD MUNZINGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/31/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 8 of the petition is missing the alternate executor and contingent beneficiary. (2) They must also receive notice. [Probate Code Section 8110.] (3) Did the testator leave a written statement or list disposing of certain items of tangible personal property? Beneficiaries of such tangible personal property, if they are not already among the beneficiaries listed in item 8 of the petition, must be identified and must be given notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/19/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Predeceased parents do not need to be listed in item 8 of a Petition for Probate when a decedent is survived by issue.)
